paper.title: The IACOB project VIII. Searching for empirical signatures of binarity in fast-rotating O-type stars

review.summary: This paper (IACOB VIII) performs a complete multi-epoch spectroscopic search for binary signatures in 54 fast-rotating (vsini>200 km/s) Galactic O-type stars, complemented by Gaia proper motions, TESS photometry, and literature data. The paper contains 7 internal LaTeX tables: (1) main sample catalog of 50 fast rotators with vsini, RV variability, SB tags, TESS photometric variability, X-ray flux, visual companions, and final binary status (Table b_fast); (2) statistical comparison of slow vs fast rotators (Table fast_stat); (3) detailed orbital parameters for 9 confirmed SB1 systems (Table sb1_fast); (4) observation log with per-instrument spectrum counts (Table sample); (5) physical parameters (Teff, logL, R, vcrit, RUWE, distances) for the sample (Table phys_parameters); (6) visual companion details (Table info_imaging); (7) SB1 slow-rotator eclipsing/ellipsoidal variables (Table tab-app-eb-ev). External resources: CDS for RV measurements tables, TESS MAST archive, ESO-FEROS archive, and XMM-Newton source catalog.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: primary sample catalog — lists 50 fast-rotating O-type stars with compiled multi-epoch spectroscopic, TESS photometric, Gaia astrometric, X-ray, and visual companion information, ordered by increasing vsini

source_refs.caption: Basic information about working sample of fast-rotating O-type stars extracted from the multi-epoch spectroscopy, TESS and Gaia data, as well as some other specific studies from the literature. The list of targets is ordered by increasing vsini.
source_refs.label: table:b_fast

columns.name: Name
columns.meaning: Star identifier (HD, BD, or ALS designation)

columns.name: SpC
columns.meaning: Spectral classification from GOSC v4.1 (Maíz Apellániz et al. 2013, 2016; Sota et al. 2011, 2014)

columns.name: vsini
columns.meaning: Projected rotational velocity from multi-epoch line-broadening analysis using iacob-broad tool; mean of all-epoch measurements

columns.name: RV_PP
columns.meaning: Peak-to-peak amplitude of radial velocity variability measured from He I 5875 line via cross-correlation

columns.name: sigma_RV
columns.meaning: Standard deviation of all radial velocity measurements per star

columns.name: SB tag
columns.meaning: Spectroscopic binary classification from visual inspection of He I 5875 line-profile variability: LPV (line profile variable / likely single), SB1, SB2, LPV/SB1?, LPV/SB2?

columns.name: Phot. var.
columns.meaning: TESS photometric variability type: SLF (stochastic low frequency), SPB/beta Cep (coherent pulsations), EB (eclipsing binary), EV (ellipsoidal variable), RM (reflection modulation), rot? (possible rotational modulation), PQ (poor quality), fr>5 (prominent peaks above 5/d), underscore (no data)

columns.name: X-ray
columns.meaning: Relative X-ray flux log(L_X/L_BOL); references given in footnotes

columns.name: Contamination
columns.meaning: Number of detected visual companions within 1 arcmin and between 1-2 arcmin, separated by '+' sign; based on Gaia-EDR3, WDSC, SMASH, AstraLux

columns.name: Runaway?
columns.meaning: Runaway star status: yes/no from Gaia-EDR3 proper motions (v_t,lsr threshold 30 km/s) and literature

columns.name: Final binary status
columns.meaning: Final spectroscopic binary classification after combining all empirical information (spectroscopy, TESS, literature): LS (likely single), SB1, LPV/SB1?, LPV/SB2?; (*) marks revised status from column 5; (dagger) marks systems where Mahy+2022 detected faint secondary via disentangling

internal_tables.evidence: Primary data table spanning 50 fast-rotating O stars (vsini>200 km/s) with 11 columns integrating spectroscopic, photometric, astrometric, X-ray, and imaging information; this is the core observational catalog of the paper.
internal_tables.comments: Three stars in this table excluded from final statistics (BD+60deg134, HD124979, ALS12370) due to V>10 or d>3 kpc. The 4 SB2 systems with at least one fast-rotating component are listed at bottom of Table sample, not in this table.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: statistical summary comparing number and percentage of SB1, SB2, LPV/SB2?, and LS stars (plus runaways) between slow-rotating (vsini<200 km/s) and fast-rotating (vsini>200 km/s) samples after distance/magnitude cleaning

source_refs.caption: Some statistics of interest for the cleaned slow- and fast-rotating samples (i.e. excluding three stars with V>10 and/or d>3 kpc from the initial sample, see text).
source_refs.label: table:fast_stat

columns.name: Category
columns.meaning: Row label: Total, SB1, SB2, LPV/SB2?, LS (likely single)

columns.name: N_slow_all
columns.meaning: Number of stars in slow-rotating sample (vsini<200 km/s, all)

columns.name: pct_slow_all
columns.meaning: Percentage relative to total slow-rotating sample (268 stars)

columns.name: N_fast_all
columns.meaning: Number of stars in fast-rotating sample (vsini>200 km/s, all)

columns.name: pct_fast_all
columns.meaning: Percentage relative to total fast-rotating sample (51 stars)

columns.name: N_fast_runaway
columns.meaning: Number of runaway stars among fast-rotating subsample

columns.name: pct_fast_runaway
columns.meaning: Percentage of runaways relative to fast-rotating subsample (51 total, or 9 for SB1, 38 for LS)

internal_tables.evidence: Summary statistics table: 268 slow vs 51 fast rotators. Key results: SB1 13% vs 18%; SB2 33% vs 8%; LS 54% vs 70%; fast rotator runaways ~47% (64% of LS subgroup).
internal_tables.comments: Slow-rotator percentages are lower/upper limits due to some stars having only 1-2 spectra; LPV/SB2? stars (n=2) not included in SB2 count.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: detailed orbital and physical parameters for the 9 confirmed SB1 systems among fast rotators, including newly discovered ones; serves as follow-up characterization of binary subpopulation

source_refs.caption: Some information of interest for the nine 'bona fide' SB1 systems found among the fast-rotating sample, ordered by increasing vsini estimates.
source_refs.label: table:sb1_fast

columns.name: Target
columns.meaning: Star identifier (HD designation)

columns.name: SpC
columns.meaning: Spectral classification

columns.name: vsini
columns.meaning: Projected rotational velocity

columns.name: RVpp
columns.meaning: Peak-to-peak radial velocity amplitude

columns.name: Teff
columns.meaning: Effective temperature

columns.name: log(L/Lsun)_spec
columns.meaning: Spectroscopic luminosity parameter log(L/Lsun) from Holgado+2020

columns.name: R
columns.meaning: Stellar radius from Gaia-EDR3 distances

columns.name: log(L/Lsun)
columns.meaning: Bolometric luminosity

columns.name: M_sp
columns.meaning: Spectroscopic mass from surface gravity and radius

columns.name: vsini/vcrit
columns.meaning: Ratio of projected rotational velocity to critical velocity

columns.name: Runaway tag
columns.meaning: Runaway status (y/n)

columns.name: EB tag
columns.meaning: Eclipsing binary / photometric variability tag (EB, RM, ?, n)

columns.name: P (orbital period)
columns.meaning: Orbital period from literature

columns.name: K1
columns.meaning: Semi-amplitude of primary radial velocity curve

columns.name: e
columns.meaning: Orbital eccentricity

columns.name: f(m)
columns.meaning: Mass function

columns.name: i
columns.meaning: Orbital inclination angle

columns.name: v_sync
columns.meaning: Surface velocity if spin-orbit synchronized

columns.name: v_eq
columns.meaning: Estimated equatorial velocity assuming tabulated inclination

columns.name: M2_from_Msp
columns.meaning: Rough mass estimate of hidden secondary from spectroscopic mass, f(m), and i

columns.name: M2_from_Mev
columns.meaning: Rough mass estimate of hidden secondary from evolutionary mass (~25+/-5 Msun), f(m), and i

internal_tables.evidence: Table compiles orbital solutions from Mahy+2022, Johnston+2021, Cazorla+2017a, Trigueros Páez+2021, and this work; two newly confirmed SB1 systems (HD152200, HD46485).
internal_tables.comments: Horizontal format: 9 stars across columns with parameters in rows. Orbital parameters sourced from Mahy+2022 except where noted.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: observation log listing number of available spectra per instrument (FEROS, FIES, HERMES, STELLA), total usable spectra (SNR_L>5), and time-span for all 54 fast rotators

source_refs.caption: Number of available spectra and total time-span of the compiled spectroscopic observations for the main working sample of 50 fast-rotating Galactic O-type stars ordered by increasing the vsini estimates. For completeness, the four SB2 systems identified as having at least one of the components with a vsini larger than 200 km/s are quoted at the bottom of the table.
source_refs.label: table:sample

columns.name: Name
columns.meaning: Star identifier

columns.name: SpC
columns.meaning: Spectral classification from Sota+2011,2014 and Maíz Apellániz+2016

columns.name: N_FEROS
columns.meaning: Number of FEROS spectra obtained

columns.name: N_FIES
columns.meaning: Number of FIES spectra obtained

columns.name: N_HERMES
columns.meaning: Number of HERMES spectra obtained

columns.name: N_STELLA
columns.meaning: Number of STELLA/SES spectra obtained

columns.name: N_total
columns.meaning: Total number of spectra with SNR_L>5 (usable for RV analysis)

columns.name: Time-span
columns.meaning: Total time baseline covered by multi-epoch observations

internal_tables.evidence: Observations from FIES@NOT (R~46000/25000), HERMES@Mercator (R~85000), FEROS@MPG/ESO2.2m (R~48000), STELLA/SES@Izaña; supplemented with ESO-FEROS archive spectra.
internal_tables.comments: Bottom 4 rows list the 4 clearly-detected SB2 systems with at least one fast-rotating component: HD175514, HD165921, HD100213, HDE228854.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: fundamental physical parameters for the 50 fast-rotating stars derived from spectroscopy and Gaia-EDR3 distances, used for sHRD placement and evolutionary interpretation

source_refs.caption: Basic information about fundamental physical parameters of our sample of fast-rotating O-type stars ordered by increasing the vsini estimates. Extracted from Holgado PhD and Holgado+2020.
source_refs.label: table:phys_parameters

columns.name: Name
columns.meaning: Star identifier

columns.name: Teff
columns.meaning: Effective temperature from iacob-gbat quantitative spectroscopic analysis (Holgado+2020)

columns.name: log(L/Lsun)
columns.meaning: Spectroscopic luminosity parameter L = Teff^4/g_c (g_c = surface gravity corrected for centrifugal forces); from Holgado+2020

columns.name: R
columns.meaning: Stellar radius derived from extinction-corrected V magnitude (Maíz Apellániz & Barbá 2018) and Gaia-EDR3 distance (Bailer-Jones+2021)

columns.name: log(L/Lsun)_bol
columns.meaning: Bolometric luminosity

columns.name: v_crit
columns.meaning: Critical rotational velocity

columns.name: vsini/v_crit
columns.meaning: Ratio of projected rotational velocity to critical velocity

columns.name: RUWE
columns.meaning: Gaia-EDR3 renormalised unit weight error (quality flag: >1.4 indicates problematic astrometric solution)

columns.name: distBJ
columns.meaning: Median geometric distance from Bailer-Jones+2021 using Gaia-EDR3 parallaxes

internal_tables.evidence: Parameters extracted from Holgado PhD thesis and Holgado+2020; used throughout the paper for sHRD placement and evolutionary interpretation.
internal_tables.comments: Uncertainties in brackets = distance uncertainty; those without brackets = iacob-gbat analysis uncertainty.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: supplementary table listing detected visual companions within 2 arcmin for TESS contamination assessment; includes SB, EB, and runaway status for cross-reference

source_refs.caption: Information about visible components for our sample of fast rotators. Only those cases with detected companions within 2 arcmin and having a difference in magnitude smaller than 3 mag are quoted in this table.
source_refs.label: table:info_imaging

columns.name: Name
columns.meaning: Star identifier

columns.name: SB?
columns.meaning: Spectroscopic binary classification (LS, SB1, LPV/SB2?)

columns.name: EB?
columns.meaning: Eclipsing binary status (EB or n)

columns.name: RW?
columns.meaning: Runaway status (y or n)

columns.name: N_comp_inner
columns.meaning: Number of visual companions within 1 arcmin

columns.name: deltaV_inner
columns.meaning: Difference in V magnitude for companion within 1 arcmin

columns.name: ang_dist_inner
columns.meaning: Angular distance for companion within 1 arcmin

columns.name: N_comp_outer
columns.meaning: Number of visual companions between 1 and 2 arcmin

columns.name: deltaV_outer
columns.meaning: Difference in V magnitude for companion between 1-2 arcmin

columns.name: ang_dist_outer
columns.meaning: Angular distance for companion between 1-2 arcmin

internal_tables.evidence: Data compiled from Gaia-EDR3, Washington Double Star Catalog (Mason+2001), SMASH survey (Sana+2014), AstraLux, and FGS; used to assess TESS light curve contamination.
internal_tables.comments: Companions with delta_mag>3 but separation<2 arcsec also included. Asterisk (*) marks companions detected by Sana+2014 (SMASH).

internal_tables.role_in_paper: supplementary comparison sample — lists 7 SB1 stars in the slow-rotating domain (vsini<200 km/s) with detected TESS eclipses or ellipsoidal/reflection modulation, used for companion nature discussion

source_refs.caption: Some information of interest for the SB1 stars in the low vsini sample for which we have detected signatures of eclipses or ellipsoidal/reflection modulation in their TESS light curves.
source_refs.label: tab-app-eb-ev

columns.name: ID
columns.meaning: Star identifier (HD or BD designation)

columns.name: SpC
columns.meaning: Spectral classification

columns.name: vsini
columns.meaning: Projected rotational velocity

columns.name: RVpp
columns.meaning: Peak-to-peak radial velocity amplitude

columns.name: P
columns.meaning: Orbital period

columns.name: Phot. var. tag
columns.meaning: TESS photometric variability classification: EB (eclipsing binary), EV (ellipsoidal variable), EV/RM (ellipsoidal or reflection modulation)

internal_tables.evidence: Includes Cyg X-1 (HD226868), plus HD12323 and HD94024 proposed as quiescent black hole candidates; HD53975 needs more epochs for period determination.
internal_tables.comments: This table supports the discussion about hidden companion nature (BH/NS candidates) in the slow-rotating domain.

external_resources.description: CDS VizieR catalog service for A&A journal — individual RV measurements and RVpp estimates for each star available in electronic form only

source_refs.context: Tables xx and xx with individual RV measurements for each star and RVpp estimates are only available in electronic form at the CDS via anonymous ftp to cdsarc.cds.unistra.fr (130.79.128.5) or via https://cdsarc.cds.unistra.fr/cgi-bin/qcat?J/A+A/

external_resources.evidence: Paper explicitly states that electronic tables with individual RV measurements per star and per-epoch RVpp values are available at CDS.
external_resources.comments: The exact J/A+A/ catalog number is not specified in the paper text; final catalog ID determined upon publication.

external_resources.description: Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes (MAST) — source of TESS 2-min short-cadence and 30-min long-cadence light curves used for photometric variability analysis and eclipsing binary search

source_refs.context: we first searched and extracted the TESS 2-min short-cadence data from the Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes (MAST, https://archive.stsci.edu/)

external_resources.evidence: TESS data extracted from MAST using lightkurve package; 39 light curves obtained (20 from short-cadence, 21 from long-cadence via full-frame images).

external_resources.description: ESO FEROS archive — supplementary high-resolution spectra downloaded to complement OWN survey observations for southern targets

source_refs.context: these observations were complemented with a few spectra downloaded from the ESO-FEROS archive (http://archive.eso.org/wdb/wdb/adp/phase3_spectral/form)

external_resources.evidence: Paper states that FEROS archive spectra supplemented OWN survey multi-epoch data for southern targets.

external_resources.description: 4XMM-DR10 X-ray source catalog — X-ray spectra downloaded and analyzed with Xspec for two newly-identified X-ray sources (HD76556, HD228841) to measure log(L_X/L_BOL)

source_refs.context: Two additional ones, HD76556 and HD228841, are listed in the 4XMM source catalog. Their spectra were downloaded (https://xcatdb.unistra.fr/4xmmdr10/index.html) and analyzed within Xspec.

external_resources.evidence: New X-ray analysis for 2 stars using 4XMM-DR10 data; both show soft emission consistent with embedded wind-shocks (log L_X/L_BOL ~ -7).
